attention1.ptxt: Never use brake fluid other than specified.
Otherwise, brake hose and rubber parts may get damaged or brake system may not operate normally.

attention2.ptxt: Do not allow brake fluid to get on painted surfaces. Painted surfaces will be damaged by brake fluid. If any fluid is spilled, flush it with water immediately.

item.ptxt: Be sure to use brake fluid indicated on brake master cylinder reservoir cap (of the vehicle) or in owner’s manual.
Check that brake fluid level is between MAX and MIN marks on reservoir of brake master cylinder.

attention3.ptxt: If brake warning light comes on during driving or brake fluid level on brake master cylinder reservoir is “MIN” or lower, check the following points:

item.ptxt: Thickness of front brake pads and rear brake shoes (rear drum brake model)
Thickness of front brake pads and rear brake pads (rear disc brake model)
Fluid leakage from piston seal of front brake caliper
Fluid leakage from piston seal of rear brake caliper (rear disc brake model)
Fluid leakage from rear brake wheel cylinder (rear drum brake model)
Cracks or damage to brake hoses and brake pipes
Damage to clutch hose and clutch pipe (M/T model)
Fluid leakage from brake master cylinder and reservoir

attention3.ptxt: In case any abnormality is found, repair or replace affected parts.
After the check and repair are completed, replenish brake fluid so that fluid level is fall between “MAX” and “MIN” marks on brake master cylinder reservoir.
